steps.title: Identify what the axes represent
steps.content:
The graph shows the $xy$-plane where $x$ is the length (in meters) and $y$ is the width (in meters).

The line itself represents all valid combinations of length and width for a rectangle with a 36 m perimeter.

The constraint equation is: $2(x + y) = 36$, which simplifies to $x + y = 18$.

Every point on this line satisfies that relationship.

steps.title: Read the coordinates of the point (8, 10)
steps.content:
The point $(8, 10)$ is marked on the graph. In an ordered pair $(x, y)$, the first coordinate is the $x$-value and the second is the $y$-value. Therefore:
  • $x = 8$ meters (length)
  • $y = 10$ meters (width)
We can verify this point is on the line: $8 + 10 = 18$ ✓

A rectangle with length 8 m and width 10 m has perimeter $2(8 + 10) = 36$ m, which matches the problem constraint. That matches choice C — C is correct.
